---
title: "Quest 14: Functions Part 2 - Non-Returning"
subtitle: "Use Functions for Actions"
format:
  live-html:
    code-tools: true
---

::: {.quest-badge}
🧩 QUEST 14 | Difficulty: Beginner | Time: 8 minutes
:::

::: {.tip-box}
**💻 Practice Options:**
- 🦀 [Open Rust Playground](https://play.rust-lang.org/)
- 📓 [Open Notebook](../jupyterlite/content/14-functions-non-returning.ipynb)
- 📥 [Download Notebook](../files/lessons/14-functions-non-returning.ipynb)
:::

## 📖 Introduction
Some functions perform work and do not return data.
They are great for clear output and reusable routines.

## 💡 Example

```rust
fn show_banner(name: &str) {
    println!("====================");
    println!("Welcome, {}!", name);
    println!("====================");
}

fn main() {
    show_banner("Rust Learner");
}
```

## 🎯 Gentle Challenge
Write a function that prints three study tips.
Keep each tip short and positive.

## ✅ One Possible Solution

```rust
fn print_tips() {
    println!("1) Read compiler messages fully");
    println!("2) Change one thing at a time");
    println!("3) Practice daily");
}

fn main() {
    print_tips();
}
```

::: {.quest-complete}
### ✨ Quest 14 Complete
You used functions for clean side-effect actions.
Next: [Quest 15: Returning Functions](15-functions-returning.qmd)
:::
